title: Learn from Failure: How to Build Resilience
description: Let's learn from failure with Gal-chan in a fun way!

scenes.speech: Failure is the path to success! Let's learn about resilience!
scenes.title: Learn from Failure: How to Build Resilience

scenes.speech: Let's learn from failure with Gal-chan!
scenes.title: Failure is a 'Discovery'
scenes.content: Edison failed over 10,000 times while developing the light bulb. 'I haven't failed. I've just found 10,000 ways that don't work.' - This quote says it all!
scenes.highlight: Edison: Experiment log with over 10,000 failure records. All recorded as 'data for the next try'

scenes.speech: Let's learn from failure with Gal-chan!
scenes.title: The 'Failure Culture' of Silicon Valley
scenes.content: In Silicon Valley, failure is a 'badge of honor'. Entrepreneurs who have experienced failure once have a higher success rate next time! In Japan, the culture of 'one failure and you're done' hinders entrepreneurship. It's essential to create a culture that doesn't fear failure.
scenes.highlight: US Entrepreneurial Failure Experience: VCs tend to invest in entrepreneurs with prior failure experience

scenes.speech: Let's learn from failure with Gal-chan!
scenes.title: The 'Post-Mortem' Technique
scenes.content: After failing, 'post-mortem' analysis means calmly analyzing 'why it failed'. ① What happened ② Why it happened ③ What to do next - Writing down these three steps turns failure into 'assets'!
scenes.highlight: Post-Mortem: Google's project failure review method. Not criticism, but improvement is the goal

scenes.speech: Let's learn from failure with Gal-chan!
scenes.title: Building Resilience (Mental Recovery)
scenes.content: The ability to recover from difficulties, 'resilience', is not a talent, but a skill that can be developed through practice. ① Acknowledge emotions ② Seek support ③ Accumulate small successes ④ 'This is also learning' - 4 steps!
scenes.highlight: Resilience Research: Childhood 'moderate difficulty experiences' increase adult resilience

scenes.speech: Let's learn from failure with Gal-chan!
scenes.title: Don't Fear Pivoting (Changing Direction)
scenes.content: When you see market reactions and judge that 'this approach won't work', it's essential to change direction (pivot) early. Instagram was originally a location-based game app! YouTube was originally a dating video service!
scenes.highlight: Famous Pivot Examples: Slack (game company → communication tool) · YouTube (dating video → general video)

scenes.speech: Let's learn from failure with Gal-chan!
scenes.title: Mental Health and Entrepreneurs
scenes.content: Many entrepreneurs are mentally strained by the pressure of starting a business. What's essential is ① taking moderate rest ② talking to peers ③ consulting experts ④ acknowledging 'it's okay not to be perfect'. Protecting yourself is the key to long-term success!
scenes.highlight: Entrepreneurial Depression: Higher prevalence than the general population. Mental healthcare directly leads to entrepreneurial success

scenes.speech: Let's learn from failure with Gal-chan!
scenes.title: Growth Mindset
scenes.content: Fixed mindset: 'Talent is fixed' vs. Growth mindset: 'Effort can lead to growth'. Carol Dweck's research proves that people with a growth mindset are stronger in the face of difficulties and achieve more!
scenes.highlight: Growth Mindset: Stanford University's Dweck Professor's research. Positive perception of challenges, failures, and efforts

scenes.speech: Let's learn from failure with Gal-chan!
scenes.title: Your 'Why' to Keep Going
scenes.content: Startups can overcome difficulties because they have a 'strong Why'. Not just for money. 'I want to change the world' or 'I want to help someone' - Passion is the strongest engine!
scenes.highlight: Simon Sinek 'Start with Why': Great organizations and leaders act from their Why

scenes.speech: Master resilience! Next, start a digital business 🎉
scenes.title: Lesson Complete!
